sections.heading: Complications
sections.content: {"disease_related": [{"complication": "Metastatic disease (lung, bone, liver, lymph nodes, adrenal gland, brain)", "management": "Systemic therapy, surgery (metastasectomy), radiation therapy (SBRT), or active surveillance depending on volume, location, and symptoms."}, {"complication": "Skeletal-related events (SREs) from bone metastases", "management": "Bone-modifying agents (denosumab or bisphosphonates like zoledronic acid), palliative radiation therapy (SBRT preferred), and pain management."}, {"complication": "Brain metastases (occurs in ~2-8% of advanced RCC)", "management": "Stereotactic radiosurgery (SRS) or fractionated stereotactic radiation therapy (SRT) for limited metastases; whole-brain radiation therapy (WBRT) only for palliative purposes when SRS/SRT is not feasible."}], "treatment_related": {"chemotherapy": ["Myelosuppression", "Nausea", "Fatigue", "Hepatotoxicity"], "immunotherapy": ["Immune-mediated adverse events (colitis, hepatitis, pneumonitis, endocrinopathies, rash)", "Fatigue", "Hypothyroidism"], "targeted_therapy": ["Hypertension", "Diarrhea", "Palmar-plantar erythrodysesthesia syndrome", "Hepatotoxicity (elevated transaminases)", "Fatigue", "Hypothyroidism", "Proteinuria", "Stomatitis"], "surgery": ["Chronic kidney disease", "Cardiovascular morbidity and mortality", "Lymphocele", "Bleeding", "Infection"], "radiation": ["Gastrointestinal toxicity (for abdominal SBRT)", "Nephrotoxicity", "Cognitive decline (after WBRT)"]}}

labels: Metastatic disease (lung, bone, liver, lymph nodes, adrenal gland, brain)
Brain metastases (occurs in ~2-8% of advanced RCC)
Immune-mediated adverse events (colitis, hepatitis, pneumonitis, endocrinopathies, rash)

cards.name: Metastatic disease (lung, bone, liver, lymph nodes, adrenal gland, brain)
cards.detail: Systemic therapy, surgery (metastasectomy), radiation therapy (SBRT), or active surveillance depending on volume, location, and symptoms.

cards.name: Skeletal-related events (SREs) from bone metastases
cards.detail: Bone-modifying agents (denosumab or bisphosphonates like zoledronic acid), palliative radiation therapy (SBRT preferred), and pain management.

cards.name: Brain metastases (occurs in ~2-8% of advanced RCC)
cards.detail: Stereotactic radiosurgery (SRS) or fractionated stereotactic radiation therapy (SRT) for limited metastases; whole-brain radiation therapy (WBRT) only for palliative purposes when SRS/SRT is not feasible.

cards.name: Immune-mediated adverse events (colitis, hepatitis, pneumonitis, endocrinopathies, rash)
cards.detail: Immune-mediated adverse events (colitis, hepatitis, pneumonitis, endocrinopathies, rash)
